opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: It feels like my whole stomach is cramped and burning, I cannot even stand up straight or move.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly a few hours ago and has got much worse — he can't get up, he's been sick twice, and my spouse is really worried.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on abruptly about 4 hours ago
symptom_narrative.character: started as a dull ache, then quickly turned into a sharp, constant, burning/cramping pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain he's ever had — he can't stand up straight or move
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all across the stomach, feels like the whole belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely still and taking very shallow breaths
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement, shifting position, or taking a deep breath makes it much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling sick with two episodes of vomiting, and he feels feverish and sweaty
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely stuck in bed, cannot get up or walk

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home with spouse, who is present and made the call and is very anxious

objective_data_offered.value: lying curled up on his side, completely still, looks sweaty and flushed, breathing in a shallow rapid way and clearly in a lot of distress
spouse says he feels hot to touch and has vomited twice; he won't move because it hurts too much

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective fever, no thermometer reading offered
